question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that integrase sequences were determined using patient-derived integrase coding sequences, indicating that HIV sequences from patient samples were reported.
sentences: "Genotypic resistance measurements were performed using the GeneSeqR Integrase assay, in which patient-derived integrase coding sequences were determined using conventional dye-deoxy chain terminator chemistry (ABI, Foster City, CA)."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper describes phenotypic susceptibility testing to raltegravir, which involves measuring the fold-change in IC50 of the sample virus compared to a reference strain, indicating in vitro drug susceptibility data.
sentences: "Phenotypic susceptibility to raltegravir was determined using PhenoSense® Integrase. Briefly, integrase sequences were amplified from patient plasma by RT-PCR and transferred into a resistance test vector containing a luciferase reporter gene. Pseudotyped virus containing patient-derived integrase was used to infect HEK293 cells in the presence and absence of serial dilutions of raltegravir. Susceptibility was reported as the fold-change in IC50 (ratio of IC50 of the sample virus to the IC50 of a reference strain [NL4-3])."
